'''Chris Beattie''' (born 26 August 1975 in [[Ipswich, Queensland]]) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played for [[Lézignan Sangliers]] in the [[Elite One Championship]] [[rugby league]] competition in [[France]].

==Playing career==
Beattie made his debut in the [[National Rugby League]] in 2000, playing for the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], as a 24-year-old.

A regular in the Sharks side, Beattie went on to represent [[Queensland Maroons|Queensland]] in the [[Rugby League State of Origin|State of Origin]], playing two games in 2001 and one in 2002.<ref>{{cite web|publisher=FOGS|title=Chris Beattie|url=http://www.fogs.com.au/players/a-f-1/chris-beattie|accessdate=7 April 2009}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/a-history-of-heartbreak-cronulla-sharks-tale-of-woe-in-nrl-finals-20160916-grib48.html/|title=A history of heartbreak: Cronulla Sharks' tale of woe in NRL finals|website=The Sydney Morning Herald}}</ref>

Beattie was signed by the [[Perpignan]]-based [[Catalans Dragons]] in their inaugural [[Super League]] season in 2006, after five seasons with the Sharks. 
 
He signed a one-year deal with the [[Sydney City Roosters]] for 2007,<ref>{{cite news|publisher=Fox Sports News (Australia)|title=Young Roosters ready to fire|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,20832064-23210,00.html|accessdate=7 April 2009|date=27 November 2006}}</ref> and in 2008 he joined the [[Lézignan Sangliers]], a French rugby league club.
